program_source.note: Master Follow-Up Program brochure: defines the 5-letter Master Follow-Up set, the 3-letter Referral/Agent set, estimate-follow-up, and the experience-management (CSI->EMI) framing the numbered pieces operationalize.

trigger_tree.perfect_score: Customer rates the repair experience 100% on the ACRB survey. Appreciation + continued exposure; seeds referral development.
trigger_tree.recommend_agent: Survey identifies customer dissatisfaction with their insurance agent; shop offers assistance finding a new one. (Agent-Contact program letter set; not represented by a numbered piece in the 2021-09-07 batch.)
trigger_tree.totaled_vehicle: Insurer elected not to repair (total loss); customer never got to repair with the shop. Thank-you + vehicle-valuation assistance to retain loyalty and dealership relationships.
trigger_tree.estimate_followup: Customer-rights / estimate education follow-up (e.g. "must I obtain three estimates?"); keeps the shop top-of-mind on consumer-rights authority during the estimate phase.

variant_legend.envelope: Window/outer envelope carrier for the piece (often carries a teaser line, e.g. "Your Repair Warranty Enclosed").
variant_legend.survey: Enclosed ACRB satisfaction survey form with Online Security Code + Survey ID.

pieces.name: Total-Loss Thank-You (Vehicle Valuation Assistance)
pieces.trigger: Insurer declined to repair the vehicle (total loss); customer would have chosen this shop. Thank-you + vehicle valuation guidance.
pieces.notes: Letter body confirms total-loss framing ("The insurance company elected not to repair your vehicle") plus a Vehicle Valuation Assistance insert. No warranty/survey variant (no repair performed).

pieces.name: Thank-You + Warranty Enclosed
pieces.trigger: Repair completed. Personal thank-you, satisfaction assurance, and enclosed comprehensive repair warranty.
pieces.notes: Base post-repair warranty/thank-you letter. Warranty cert is ACRB-registered (Registered ID ACRB####### - PS####). No envelope variant in this batch.

pieces.name: Thank-You + Warranty Enclosed (variant B)
pieces.trigger: Repair completed (alternate shop/branding variant of 04). Thank-you + enclosed warranty.
pieces.notes: Same trigger as 04 with an added envelope variant; sample shows multiple recipients/shops per batch page (e.g. Marina Auto Body). 'b' suffix = layout/branding variant, not a different trigger.

pieces.name: Thank-You + Warranty + Survey Notice (full mailing)
pieces.trigger: Repair completed. Thank-you + enclosed warranty + notice that an ACRB satisfaction survey is coming (with online code). Initiates the survey cycle.
pieces.notes: Only piece in the batch with all four variants. Letter P.S. announces the ACRB survey; survey variant carries Online Security Code + Survey ID. This piece kicks off the survey -> (10 reminder | 10b responder) branch.

pieces.name: Survey Non-Responder Reminder
pieces.trigger: ACRB survey was sent but not returned. Asks the customer again to complete it (online or by mail).
pieces.notes: Body confirms non-responder framing ("We noticed that you have not returned their survey"). Branch sibling of 10b.

pieces.name: Survey Responder Thank-You / Perfect-Score + Referral Ask
pieces.trigger: Customer completed the ACRB survey (positive / perfect score). Thanks them, cites high satisfaction ranking, and asks for referrals.
pieces.notes: Body confirms responder framing ("Thank you for taking the time to complete the satisfaction survey...you can recommend us to your friends and family"). This is the Perfect-Score / referral-development letter in the triggered tree.

pieces.name: Driver's License Renewal Reminder
pieces.trigger: Time-based: customer's driver's license is near renewal (derived from repair records). Soft-touch loyalty contact.
pieces.notes: Courtesy reminder ("your license is up for renewal this coming October"). Keeps the shop visible between repairs.

pieces.name: Free Paint Maintenance Check-Up Offer
pieces.trigger: Post-repair time-based: offers a free 6-month paint maintenance check-up (wash/wax/inspection) for 18 months, tied to the lifetime paint warranty.
pieces.notes: Drives re-visits and reinforces the paint warranty. Body: "FREE paint maintenance check-up every 6 months for 18 months."

pieces.name: One-Year Anniversary Follow-Up
pieces.trigger: Time-based: ~1 year after the repair. Reaffirms lifetime warranty and offers continued assistance.
pieces.notes: Body: "it's been about a year since you brought your...in for repair." Retention / repeat-business contact.

pieces.name: Consumer Rights / Estimate-Followup Education
pieces.trigger: Estimate-phase / consumer-rights education (three-estimates myth, drive-in claims, who guarantees the repair). Positions the shop as the consumer's advocate.
pieces.notes: Maps to the Estimate-Followup branch of the triggered tree. Body answers "Must I obtain three estimates?" and related consumer-rights questions.

pieces.name: Repeat / Referral Appreciation (Past Patronage)
pieces.trigger: Time-based loyalty: acknowledges past patronage (e.g. 2 years prior), thanks for referrals, positions the shop as a vehicle-ownership resource.
pieces.notes: Body: "Your past patronage in September of 2019 is remembered with appreciation...thank you...for any associates that you have referred." Repeat + referral nurture.

pieces.name: Birthday / Seasonal Greeting
pieces.trigger: Calendar-based: birthday and seasonal/holiday greetings (multiple birthday greetings + seasonal contacts per the program brochure).
pieces.notes: Listed in spec §4 (the `b` piece) and described in the Master Follow-Up Program brochure (samples/001.md: "multiple birthday greetings, seasonal contacts"). NO sample file is present in the 2021-09-07 production batch; cataloged from the program definition only. Variants unknown until a sample batch is supplied (operator read-bridge).

gaps_and_notes: The `b` (birthday/seasonal) piece has no sample in the 2021-09-07 batch; cataloged from program docs only.

Recommend-Agent / Customer-Call-Your-Agent / Agent-Customer-Acknowledgement triggers from the triggered-letter tree are Agent-Contact program letters and are NOT represented by a numbered piece in this batch; only the customer-facing numbered pieces are present.
Piece 04 has no envelope variant in this batch; 04b adds the envelope. All other repaired-customer pieces carry an envelope.

`program_trigger` values map each piece to the triggered-letter tree where a board-named trigger applies (perfect_score, recommend_agent, totaled_vehicle, estimate_followup); base/time-based/calendar pieces use descriptive trigger keys.
